title: podman stop <container> --time 0 resulted in "Error: given PIDs did not die within timeout"
body:
### Issue Description

We have a podman container running inside a Qemu instance (docker instance). 
podman stop <container> -time 0 on the Qemu resulted in 
Error: given PIDs did not die within timeout
making the container in accessible.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run -t --rm <image> /sbin/init <params>
2. podman stop <container> -time 0

Very rare to reproduce and when occurred makes container in accessible.

### Describe the results you received

Error: given PIDs did not die within timeout

### Describe the results you expected

<container name>

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Issue happens very rare and is very hard to reproduce. 
We do see it on 3.4.3 version we have inhouse.

comments.body:
```
$ podman run -t --rm /sbin/init 
```
There is no image specified here^^

Was the image properly setup to run with /sbin/init?  Does it have the correct stop signal?

Can you get a little more modern Podman?

Hi @rhatdan ,
Here is the full podman run command.. 
IMG_NAME points to an oci container built by yocto 

/usr/bin/podman run -t --network=host --rm --name $1 \
--hostname=$hostname \
--systemd=always \
-v /dev:/dev \
-v /var/run/dbus:/var/run/host-dbus \
-v /mnt:/mnt/host:rshared \
-v /run/podman/podman.sock:/run/podman/podman.sock \
--device=/dev/packet_injector:/dev/packet_injector \
--device=/dev/ppp:/dev/ppp:rw \
--shm-size=1g --privileged --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs \
--security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
$IMG_NAME /sbin/init root=/dev/vda rw highres=off \
mem=1024M ip=dhcp vga=0 uvesafb.mode_option=640x480-32 oprofile.timer=1 \
uvesafb.task_timeout=-1 CARD_ID=$CARD_ID \
$options \
systemdParams="" > /dev/null

Yes the image is properly setup to run with /sbin/init which starts systemd as PID 1 inside container.

The current version of linux we have can only support upto podman 3.4.3.
Please try with a more recent version of Podman.  Upstream we cannot support such an old version.

@vrothberg sure we will update the podman
Can you help us identify what scenarios we might run into these errors?

> Can you help us identify what scenarios we might run into these errors?

It's probably just a bug in `podman stop` when using -t0 (in version v3.4.3).  In that case you may just use `podman kill`.
I am going to close but we can continue the conversation.
